description: Refactor workflow engine to support non-agentic action transitions, add story workflow template, and thread worktree management into the engine so agents land directly in their worktree.

subtasks.description:
Add action dispatch to WorkflowEngine.mag so transitions can have a built-in 'action' field instead of a 'role'.

Files to modify: src/dispatcher/WorkflowEngine.mag

Currently, when the engine fires a transition with a 'role', it writes a task tuple for the TaskDispatcher. We need a new path: if a transition has an 'action' key (and no 'role'), the engine executes a built-in operation directly — no agent spawned.

1. In the advance method, where transitions are fired, add a check before creating a task:
   - action := transition at: 'action' ifAbsent: [nil].
   - action notNil ifTrue: [self executeAction: action transition: transition instance: instanceId. ^self].
   - Otherwise fall through to existing task creation logic.

2. Add method executeAction: action transition: transition instance: instanceId
   - This is a dispatch table. For now, implement it as a series of ifTrue: checks:
     action = 'create-worktree' ifTrue: [^self actionCreateWorktree: transition instance: instanceId].
     action = 'merge-worktree' ifTrue: [^self actionMergeWorktree: transition instance: instanceId].
     action = 'notify-head' ifTrue: [^self actionNotifyHead: transition instance: instanceId].
   - If action is unknown, write an observation: pp observe 'unknown-action' 'Unknown action: <action>'

3. Add three STUB methods that just log and return (actual implementations come in parallel stories):
   - actionCreateWorktree:instance: — '(stub) create-worktree' println.
   - actionMergeWorktree:instance: — '(stub) merge-worktree' println.
   - actionNotifyHead:instance: — '(stub) notify-head' println.

4. After executing an action, the engine must still consume input tokens and produce output tokens (same as agentic transitions). Make sure the action path includes this token movement.

Conventions: All temp vars at method top. No ifNil:/ifNotNil:. Follow existing code patterns in WorkflowEngine.mag.

Implement the create-worktree action in WorkflowEngine.mag.

Replace the stub actionCreateWorktree:instance: with a real implementation.

Behavior:
1. Read the repo signal to find the repo path:
   repo := bbs rdp: 'signal' scope: instanceId identity: 'repo'.
   repoPath := repo payload at: 'path'.
   If no repo signal exists, check for a PP_REPO_PATH-style convention or fall back to the current working directory.

2. Create a feature branch from main:
   featureBranch := 'feature/', instanceId.
   Run: git -C <repoPath> checkout -b <featureBranch>
   Use ExternalProcess command: '/bin/sh' with args: #('-c' <shell command>) — same pattern as Repo.mag and ClaudeHarness.mag.

3. Create a worktree for the implementation:
   worktreeDir := home, '/.pp/worktrees/', instanceId, '/impl'.
   implBranch := 'impl/', instanceId.
   Run: git -C <repoPath> worktree add <worktreeDir> -b <implBranch>
   Get home via: home := System env: 'HOME'.

4. Write a signal so subsequent transitions know where to work:
   payload := Dictionary new.
   payload at: 'workdir' put: worktreeDir.
   payload at: 'branch' put: implBranch.
   payload at: 'feature_branch' put: featureBranch.
   payload at: 'repo_path' put: repoPath.
   bbs upsertSignal: instanceId identity: 'worktree' payload: payload.

5. Also checkout main in the original repo so it's not left on the feature branch:
   Run: git -C <repoPath> checkout main

Conventions: All temp vars at method top. ExternalProcess pattern from Repo.mag. No ifNil:/ifNotNil:.

Implement the merge-worktree action in WorkflowEngine.mag.

Replace the stub actionMergeWorktree:instance: with a real implementation.

Behavior:
1. Read the worktree signal:
   info := bbs rdp: 'signal' scope: instanceId identity: 'worktree'.
   Extract: repoPath, featureBranch, implBranch, worktreeDir from info payload.

2. Merge the impl branch into the feature branch:
   Run: git -C <repoPath> checkout <featureBranch>
   Run: git -C <repoPath> merge <implBranch> --no-edit
   If merge fails, write an observation and a notification, do NOT advance — the workflow should stall so the user can intervene.

3. Clean up the worktree:
   Run: git -C <repoPath> worktree remove <worktreeDir>
   Run: git -C <repoPath> branch -d <implBranch>

4. Checkout main so the repo isn't left on the feature branch:
   Run: git -C <repoPath> checkout main

5. Write observation recording the merge:
   bbs out: 'observation' scope: instanceId identity: 'merge-complete' payload with detail of what was merged.

Error handling: Wrap git commands in [] on: Exception do: [:e | ...]. If merge conflicts, write notification with severity 'urgent' and do NOT produce output tokens (workflow stalls for human intervention).

Conventions: All temp vars at method top. ExternalProcess via /bin/sh pattern.

Implement the notify-head action in WorkflowEngine.mag.

Replace the stub actionNotifyHead:instance: with a real implementation.

Behavior:
1. Read the workflow tuple to get the description:
   workflow := bbs rdp: 'workflow' scope: 'default' identity: instanceId.
   desc := workflow notNil ifTrue: [workflow payload at: 'description' ifAbsent: [instanceId]] ifFalse: [instanceId].
   Wait — no ifTrue:ifFalse: on the result of notNil. Instead:
   desc := instanceId.
   workflow notNil ifTrue: [
     | pd |
     pd := workflow payload at: 'description' ifAbsent: [nil].
     pd notNil ifTrue: [desc := pd]
   ].

2. Read any observations from the workflow for a summary:
   observations := bbs scan: 'observation' scope: instanceId.

3. Build a notification message:
   msg := 'Workflow complete: ', desc.
   observations size > 0 ifTrue: [
     msg := msg, ' (', observations size printString, ' observations recorded)'
   ].

4. Write the notification:
   payload := Dictionary new.
   payload at: 'message' put: msg.
   payload at: 'severity' put: 'info'.
   payload at: 'workflow' put: instanceId.
   bbs out: 'notification' scope: 'system' identity: 'workflow-complete:', instanceId payload: payload.

Conventions: All temp vars at method top. No ifNil:/ifNotNil:.

Modify task creation in the workflow engine so that when a worktree signal exists for the workflow instance, the task is automatically created with the workdir from that signal. Agents then land directly in the worktree.

Files to modify: src/dispatcher/WorkflowEngine.mag (task creation in advance method)

Currently, when the engine creates a task tuple for an agentic transition, it sets role, description, status, etc. in the payload. We need to also set 'workdir' if a worktree signal exists.

In the code path that creates a task (after checking action is nil and role is present):
1. Look up worktree signal: worktreeInfo := bbs rdp: 'signal' scope: instanceId identity: 'worktree'.
2. If found, add to the task payload:
   worktreeInfo notNil ifTrue: [
     taskPayload at: 'workdir' put: (worktreeInfo payload at: 'workdir').
     taskPayload at: 'repo' put: (worktreeInfo payload at: 'repo_path').
   ].

Also verify that TaskDispatcher.mag already reads 'workdir' from the task payload when spawning the harness. Check ClaudeHarness.mag to confirm it uses the workDir parameter. If the plumbing already exists end-to-end, no changes needed there — just ensure the task payload carries the field.

Conventions: All temp vars at method top. Read existing code first to understand the task creation flow.

Add the 'story' workflow template to Main.mag seed, and rename scout-done to scout-mission.

Files to modify: src/Main.mag

1. In seedTemplates:, the scout template identity should be 'scout-mission' (check if already done — it may have been changed already). The description should be 'Scout mission: research a topic, write findings document'.

2. Add a new method seedStoryTemplate: bbs, called from seedTemplates:.

3. The story template:
   identity: 'story'
   description: 'Single-task: implement in worktree, review, integrate'
   start_places: #('request')
   terminal_places: #('done')
   transitions:
     T1: id='setup', in=#('request'), out=#('ready'), action='create-worktree'
     T2: id='implement', in=#('ready'), out=#('implemented'), role='implementer', description='{{description}}'
     T3: id='review', in=#('implemented'), out=#('reviewed'), role='reviewer', description='Review implementation for: {{description}}', preconditions=[{category:'event', identity:'task-complete:{{instance}}:task:implement'}]
     T4: id='pass', in=#('reviewed'), out=#('merging'), preconditions=[{category:'signal', identity:'verdict:{{instance}}', constraint:'{decision: "pass"}'}]
     T5: id='integrate', in=#('merging'), out=#('merged'), action='merge-worktree'
     T6: id='notify', in=#('merged'), out=#('done'), action='notify-head'
     T7: id='fail', in=#('reviewed'), out=#('fixing'), preconditions=[{category:'signal', identity:'verdict:{{instance}}', constraint:'{decision: "fix"}'}]
     T8: id='fix', in=#('fixing'), out=#('implemented'), role='fixer', description='Fix issues found in review for: {{description}}'

4. Follow the exact same Dictionary/Array construction patterns as seedFullPipeline:. Use copyWith: for arrays, Dictionary new + at:put: for dicts.

Conventions: All temp vars at method top. Follow existing seed patterns exactly.
